title: Make tk graph account for cross-epic blockers
description: tk graph <epic> currently computes waves from tasks under the epic and only counts blockers that are also in that task set. If a task is blocked_by a tick outside the epic, graph JSON can mark it agent_ready even while the external blocker is open. Update graph/wave computation or graph command logic to use all ticks for blocker status while still rendering only the selected epic's tasks.
notes: 2026-06-12 07:01 - Re-parented into epic e7c (roadmap follow-ups). Recon pointer: wave computation for tk graph lives alongside cmd/tk/cmd/graph.go and internal/query; the fix mirrors ready.go semantics — blocker status must be resolved against ALL ticks (missing blockers treated as closed) while rendering only the selected epic tasks.
acceptance_criteria: A task under epic A blocked_by an open task under epic B is not shown as agent_ready in tk graph A --json; blocked_by includes the external blocker; when external blocker closes the task becomes ready; tests cover cross-epic blocker behavior.
